Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment application appealed to the Ontario Land Tribunal (Case No. OLT-21-001809). OLT approved (final order pending fulfillment of conditions) a 19-storey residential building. The Official Plan Amendment is required to permit a tall building on the site within the Yonge and St.Clair Secondary Plan.

5 and 43 Junction Road The revised application seeks approval for the redevelopment of 5 and 43 Junction Road, proposing the construction of six residential towers with heights ranging from 15 to 35 storeys. These towers will feature mid-rise podiums that accommodate residential, commercial, and office spaces. The total gross floor area (GFA) of the site will be 135,767.76 square meters, comprising 126,453.36 square meters of residential GFA and 9,314.40 square meters of non-residential GFA. The development will include 1,882 housing units, offering a mix of one, two, and three-bedroom units. Additionally, a 0.32-hectare park will be created, which includes 1,871.1 square meters of parkland dedication and 1,182.5 square meters of landscaped open space. This park will feature a landscaped and integrated rail berm that serves both as community rail protection and a park amenity.

Zoning By-law Amendment (22 140023 WET 07 OZ) and Site Plan Control (22 140022 WET 07 SA) applications to facilitate an infill development. The applicant is proposing three new towers, measuring 35-storeys, 45-storeys, and 45-storeys. All three tower sit above an eight-storey podium.The proposed development, includes 1,621 new dwelling units, gross floor area of 109,193 square metres resulting in a density of 2.64 floor space index. The proposal would contain a total of 722 parking spaces located within an underground garage.The three existing buildings on the subject site - two apartment buildings, measuring a 14 storeys and 17 storeys and a 2-storey commercial/amenity building - are proposed to be retained.
